concepts.title: Properties of pole and polar of hyperbola
concepts.conceptBody:
Pole And Polar 

The polar of a point $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ w.r.t. the hyperbola $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}-\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$ is $\text{T}=0$, where $\text{T}\equiv \frac{\text{x}{{\text{x}}_{1}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}-\frac{\text{y}{{\text{y}}_{1}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}-1$

(i) Pole of a given line $\text{l}x+\text{my}+\text{n}=0$ w.r.t. the hyperbola $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}-\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$ is $\left( \frac{-{{\text{a}}^{2}}\text{l}}{\text{n}},\frac{-{{\text{b}}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ m}}{\text{n}} \right)$

(ii) Polar of the focus is the directrix.

(iii) Any tangent is the polar of its point of contact.

(iv) If the polar of $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ passes through $\text{Q}\left( {{\text{x}}_{2}},{{\text{y}}_{2}} \right)$ then the polar of $\text{Q}$ will pass through

$\text{P}$ and such points are said to conjugate points.

(v) If the pole of a line $lx+my+n=0$ lies on the another line ${l}'x+{m}'y+{n}'=0$, then the pole of the second line will lie on the first and such lines are said to be conjugate lines.
